sanskrit:
त्याज्यं दोषवदित्येके कर्म प्राहुर्मनीषिणः।
यज्ञदानतपःकर्म न त्याज्यमिति चापरे॥
transliteration:
tyājyaṃ doṣavad ity eke karma prāhur manīṣiṇaḥ |
yajña-dāna-tapaḥ-karma na tyājyam iti cāpare ||
literal_meaning: To be given up as evil / thus / some / action / declare / the wise / sacrifice-gift-austerity-action / not / to be given up / thus / others
contextual_meaning: Arjuna hears two conflicting inner voices: one says "drop every action, it’s all flawed," the other says "keep the actions that refine the soul."  The verse mirrors the mind’s oscillation between radical withdrawal and selective engagement when facing moral overload.
psychological_state: Decision-fatigue and splitting: the ego fragments into internal committees that polarize into "all-or-nothing" camps, preventing integrated action.
trigger_scenario: A burnout client is told by one therapist to quit the stressful job entirely, by another to keep only the pro-bono parts; she freezes, fearing any choice makes her "bad."
anchor_text: Keep the acts that purify you, drop the ones that poison you.
